meta.title: Prior-art primer
meta.subject: the prior-art scene — a comparison-cluster move

beats.narration: Prior-art places the subject against systems that occupy similar terrain — not as a winner-and-losers ranking, but dimensionally. The novelty row is the one line of difference the film argues from.

scenes.heading: Where docent stands against the field

dimensions.label: Whether the form argues for what it explains

cells.note: Content; the deck does not push back
Content; the deck does not push back
Beautiful, but the argument is the author's voiceover, not the form

novelty.statement: docent's grammar is the argument — every scene type IS a cognitive move with a depth contract that refuses to render without it.

beats.narration: Three systems occupy similar terrain: Marp, Reveal.js, and Manim. Each maps an author's input onto an audience's output.
On input, Marp and docent both ask for declarative content. Reveal asks for HTML; Manim asks for Python.
On output, Marp and Reveal land on slides. Manim renders narrated motion — closer to docent's surface, but stopping at the visual.
The novelty is the third row. None of the prior systems carry an argument in their form. Their form delivers content; the argument lives in the author's voice. Docent's grammar IS the argument.

scenes.heading: Prior-art — the comparison move with novelty

points.title: The form factor
points.body: Systems × dimensions × cells. Same or diverges, with one short claim per cell.

points.title: The argument
points.body: One row is the novelty — the line of difference the film argues from. The renderer lights it.

points.title: The position
points.body: Prior-art belongs between frame and the first structure scene — the viewer learns what's been tried before they see the system itself.

beats.narration: Prior-art's form: systems by dimensions, with cells marking same or diverges.
Its argument: the novelty row — the one line of difference — gets the accent rule and the wash. The viewer's eye lands on what the film is here to claim.
Its position: between the frame and the first structure scene. What's been tried, before what we built.
